mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls signals expanding aggregate wage income and consumption demand, which drives corporate revenue expectations higher and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in payroll-growth months are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is pinned below 0.25 percent, the zero lower bound signals an extended period of monetary accommodation and compressed discount rates, which generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as equity risk premiums contract.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in more than 50 percent of triggered months when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and increases in unemployment generate a contractionary demand shock that reduces corporate revenue forecasts; the joint occurrence predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days beyond the effect of either series alone.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month decline coinciding with UNRATE 3-month rise
failure_condition: Walk-forward Sharpe of the interaction term is not significantly negative (t-statistic greater than -1.5) or the individual series fully explain returns without interaction.

mechanism_narrative: During post-recession recoveries, month-over-month acceleration in industrial production signals rising capacity utilization and operating leverage, which translates into positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: During NBER-defined recovery months, month-over-month INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 20-day SPX returns with a hit rate exceeding 55 percent.

mechanism_narrative: A negative quarter-over-quarter growth rate in real GDP triggers a sharp repricing of recession risk, causing institutional investors to reduce equity exposure and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative GDP growth quarters are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60 percent of historical instances.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate of at least 0.2 percentage points signals rapid labor market deterioration that prompts immediate risk-off repositioning, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: UNRATE increases of at least 0.2 percentage points are followed by negative 5-day SPX returns in fewer than 55 percent of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Slowing payroll growth coinciding with an elevated federal funds rate above 2 percent creates a monetary-labor drag that suppresses corporate earnings expectations and equity valuations, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The FEDFUNDS-PAYEMS interaction term exhibits a t-statistic with absolute value below 2 in predicting 60-day returns during walk-forward regression.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in housing starts reflect strengthening residential investment and construction activity, which transmits into broad equity gains through multiplier effects on durable goods and employment, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST month-over-month increases are followed by negative 20-day SPX returns in more than 50 percent of instances during the post-housing-crisis recovery period.
